I studied engineering in São Paulo.
A firm hired me as a junior appraiser.
Later, I founded my own company and became a partner.
When did you graduate? Where did you start?
Marcos, its good to see you again! How did you get into valuation?
Well, I studied civil engineering in São Paulo and graduated in 2004.
Nice. And what was your first job?
A large firm hired me as a junior appraiser. After a few years, they promoted me to team leader.
Thats a great path. So when did you start your own company?
In 2015, I founded my own firm and became a partner. Winning my first big client was a real milestone.
Impressive. You really built something from the ground up.
Thank you. It took years of experience, but it was worth it. What about your career path?
I studied civil engineering and graduated in 2004.
After that, they promoted me to team leader.
It took years of experience, but it was worth it.

Hello, everyone. Let me tell you a little about myself and how I got here. I studied economics at a university in Toronto, and I graduated in 2006. After university, a large bank hired me as a financial analyst. I worked there for several years and gained a lot of experience in finance. Then, about ten years ago, I moved into valuation, because I wanted more contact with real projects and clients. In 2018, my firm promoted me to senior partner. Winning my first international client was a real milestone in my career. Step by step, I built the path that brought me to this congress today. Thank you for listening, and I look forward to meeting you.
